rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical cobalt-catalyzed hydrofunctionalization (hydromethoxylation/hydroalkoxylation) of alkenes proceeding via metal-hydride hydrogen atom transfer (MHAT) and radical-polar crossover
electrochemical_role: Cathodic reduction regenerates the active Co(II/I) catalyst; Co-H is generated from Ph2SiH2 and delivers H• to alkene (MHAT); the carbon radical is further oxidized (anodically or via Co) to a carbocation that is trapped by MeOH/ROH
electron_flow: Electrochemically generated low-valent Co reacts with Ph2SiH2 to form a Co-H; HAT to alkene gives a carbon radical, which is oxidized to a carbocation and trapped by the alcohol nucleophile (MeOH) to give the ether; Co is regenerated cathodically.

probe_reagents.name: CD3OD (deuterated methanol)
probe_reagents.observation: Product 13 (cyclized THF) showed <2% D incorporation at the methyl-derived position, indicating the H delivered to the alkene comes from Ph2SiH2 (Co-H) and not from the alcohol solvent

probe_reagents.name: (1-cyclopropyl-1-phenyl)alkene / vinylcyclopropane 14
probe_reagents.observation: Ring-opened product 15 (homoallyl methyl ether, 40%) obtained, consistent with a carbon-centered radical intermediate undergoing cyclopropane ring opening before C-O bond formation

probe_reagents.name: n-Bu4NBF4 electrolyte
probe_reagents.observation: Electrolyte identity affects hydromethoxylation efficiency (Figure 5e), supporting electrochemical involvement

notes: Additional mechanistic probes include CV studies (III-A), attempts at asymmetric catalysis (III-D) which failed — consistent with a free (non-Co-bound) carbocation/radical intermediate after MHAT, supporting the radical-polar crossover pathway rather than a Co-alkyl inner-sphere ionization.
